reasoning: Premarket sector breadth is mixed with low dispersion. XLF +0.55% (financials) is the only sector moving above threshold, while XLK (tech, ~30% of SPY) is flat at +0.17% and XLE flat at -0.05%. This divergence—financials nudging up while tech remains unmoved—does NOT align with a classic risk-on or risk-off rotation. SPY itself is flat at -0.01%. Single-sector leadership without XLK confirmation, combined with overall <1% dispersion, suggests insufficient sector internals clarity for a directional bias. Market breadth is neutral premarket.
key_observations: XLF (financials): +0.55% — only sector moving, but insufficient to drive rotation signal without XLK confirmation
Overall rotation: XLF slightly elevated, but tech flat + low overall dispersion = MIXED/FLAT, not a clear risk-on or risk-off signal
